tool.name: SameStr Summarize
tool.description: identfies and reports shared bacterial strains across samples using variant profile similarity
tool.command:
mkdir tax_profiles out_compare &&
#for $file in $tax_profiles: ## Naming the taxonomic profile datasets correctly is crucial and error-prone. ## The parameter help explains that the names need to match sample names, ## but command line users might not strip the file suffix they are used to, ## so we do it here just in case. (This is also important for testing.) #set $tax_basename = $file.element_identifier.split('.profile.txt')[0] ln -s '$file' 'tax_profiles/${tax_basename}.profile.txt' && #end for #for $file in $overlaps: ln -s '$file' 'out_compare/${file.element_identifier}.overlap.txt' && #end for
#for $file in $similarities: ln -s '$file' 'out_compare/${file.element_identifier}.fraction.txt' && #end for
samestr summarize --marker-dir '$database.fields.path' --input-dir out_compare/ --tax-profiles-dir tax_profiles/ --aln-pair-min-overlap $aln_pair_min_overlap --aln-pair-min-similarity $aln_pair_min_similarity

param.name: database
param.label: SameStr database
param.help: SameStr database built from MetaPhlAn or mOTUS markers

param.name: overlaps
param.label: Overlap metrics (collection output of SameStr compare)

param.name: similarities
param.label: Fractional similarities (collection output of SameStr compare)

param.name: tax_profiles
param.label: Taxonomic profiles
param.help: Taxonomic profiles generated with MetaPhlAn or mOTUS for each of the samples in the SameStr compare outputs. Note: To obtain nicely formatted reports the dataset names here should match the sample names mentioned in the overlap and similarity inputs!

param.label: Minimum overlap for comparison
param.help: Minimum number of positions that must be covered in both samples to consider their similarity reliable. Higher values are more stringent. (default: 5000).

param.label: Minimum similarity for shared strains
param.help: Samples with similarity scores above this threshold are considered the same strain. Higher values are more strict. (default: 0.999).

tool.help:
@SAMESTR_INTRO@
SameStr Summarize .................
SameStr Summarize identifies and summarizes shared bacterial strains across samples based on variant profile similarity. This module generates taxonomic co-occurrence tables at multiple taxonomic levels and provides strain sharing events. This is the last step of the SameStr workflow.
**Input Requirements**
The following inputs must be provided:
- The MetaPhlAn or mOTUS clade marker database that matches the database used to generate the input profiles. - Metrics matrices from SameStr compare containing similarity scores and overlap counts for each pair of samples. - Taxonomic profiles from MetaPhlAn or mOTUS containing relative abundance data for each sample.
**Parameters**
- Minimum overlap for comparison: Minimum number of overlapping positions which have to be covered in both alignments to evaluate alignment similarity. - Minimum similarity for shared strains: Minimum pairwise alignment similarity required to call strains as shared.
**Results and Outputs**
The tool produces three tabular outputs:
- Strain co-occurrences: Taxonomic co-occurrence counts across different taxonomic levels. Shows how many taxonomic groups are shared between each sample pair. - Strain events: Detailed strain sharing events between sample pairs. - Taxon counts: Taxon counts per sample at different taxonomic levels.
